title: Force & Newton’s Laws
subject: physics
summary: SPTG focuses on F = ma, weight, equilibrium, and simple tension or normal force ideas.
coreConcepts: If net force is zero, an object is in equilibrium or moves with constant velocity.
Weight is the gravitational force on an object.
derive: Newton’s Second Law states net force is proportional to acceleration.
Weight is the force due to gravity, so W = mg.
formulas: Net force = sum of all forces
If object is at rest or constant velocity, net force = 0

sptgSet.question: m = 2 kg, a = 5 m/s². Find F.
An object is at rest. What is the net force?
g is approximately equal to what?
Find weight of a 3 kg object using g = 10.
If net force on a body is zero, what can you say about acceleration?
A 4 kg mass accelerates at 3 m/s². Find net force.
A 20 N force acts on a 5 kg mass. Find acceleration.
A body of mass 2 kg has forces 10 N right and 4 N left. Find acceleration.
A hanging body has mass 6 kg. Find its weight using g = 10.
What happens if applied force equals friction?

hardSet.question: A 5 kg object experiences forces 20 N right, 8 N left, and 2 N left. Find acceleration.
A 10 kg body is pulled with 50 N and friction is 20 N. Find acceleration.
Find the net force needed to accelerate 12 kg at 2.5 m/s².
A 40 N net force acts on a mass and gives 5 m/s² acceleration. Find mass.
A box is pushed right with 30 N and left with 18 N. Mass = 3 kg. Find acceleration.
A body moves with constant velocity. Can forces still act on it?
hardSet.answer: Yes, but net force must be zero

hardSet.question: A 2 kg object hangs motionless from a rope. Find rope tension using g = 10.
A lift moves upward at constant velocity with a 50 kg person inside. Find apparent weight trend.
A 6 kg block has acceleration 2 m/s² left. Find net force.
If acceleration doubles while mass stays the same, what happens to force?
